comment_content: The required content varies from different semester. Generally, students need to attend some training modules and seminars (Training module is different from seminar). Most of after class tasks are essay, but some are small practice like video makeing, coding, etc. Some of them are really interesting and useful, you'd better choose the one you like.
comment_workload:
The workload is a little bit heavy for a 0-credit course. After class tasks may consume about 2 hours each semester.
The best practice is to finish your task ASAP so that you can schedule your time more flexible.

comment_content:
First term in CSE [School-Based Admission or Department-based Admission] requirements:
Meet your advisor (mandatory for COMP)
Attend department UG orientation

Training modules offered:
(Fall semester)
Video Taking and Processing Training Module (Kenneth Leung and James Cheung)
Exploring the COMP Curriculum and Planning Ahead (Alex Lam)
Basic HTML/CSS and Personal Website Building (Huiru Xiao)
Using Terminal, Command Line, and Shell Script (Gibson Lam)

(Spring semester)
Video Taking and Processing Training Module (Kenneth Leung and James Cheung)
Basic LaTeX and Overleaf (Cecia Chan)
GitHub (Desmond)
Writing Your First CV (Cindy Li and Ada Leung)
comment_teaching: Teaching ranges from seminars, workshops, advising and sharing sessions, interaction with faculty and teaching staff, and discussion with student peers or alumni.
comment_workload:
In total: 2 seminars each term (depends on the year)
Complete the team-based training module on Video Taking and Processing (mandatory for COMP)

Select and complete one of the following training modules in Fall, and another in Spring (mandatory for COMP)

Completion of modules includes:
Physical attendance at the seminar for the module
A passing score on the associated assessment
comment_grading:
Its grade for each term is PP (Permitted to Proceed). The P (Pass) grade will be given at the final term before you graduate.

Failure of fulfilling the requirement in any term may lead to an 'F' grade in COMP 4900 for that term, and be permanently recorded in the transcript.

PP (Permission to Proceed) grade will be assigned upon successful fulfilment of course requirement.

comment_content:
Contents in this course includes:
- meeting with faculty academic advisor
- attend the Department UG Orientation
- 1st term of CSE (eg Year 2 Fall for DSE): attend 1 seminar
- All in-between terms: attend 2 seminars each term for COMP students OR 1 seminar for COSC students
- Last term in CSE: No seminar required
- Fill in a graduation survey
- Might be invited to an optional casual gathering

Details: http://www.cse.ust.hk/ug/comp4900
comment_teaching:
TEACHING
Just some topics related to CSE, sometimes is interesting while sometimes is...you know what I mean.
comment_workload:
Not heavy, just 2 seminars for each semester. After the seminar, submit at least 200 words within 1 week after the seminar.

Seminars are usually scheduled on Wed 6pm-7pm. Some occasional seminars will also count for COMP4900.
Arrive to the seminar venue before 6:10pm and take attendance with student card.

You can use ENGG1010 seminar to double count. Therefore, for year 2 and year 3 students, usually just need to attend to seminar each semester.
comment_grading:
GRADING
This subject only have 3 grades: P/PP/F (Pass/Permitted to Proceed/Fail)
You must not fail this subject in order to graduate
